title: गोविन्द नामावली
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गोविन्द नामावली ॥

श्रीनिवासा गोविन्दा। श्री वेंकटेशा गोविन्दा।
भक्तवत्सला गोविन्दा। भागवतप्रिय गोविन्दा॥ १॥

नित्यनिर्मला गोविन्दा। नीलमेघश्यामा गोविन्दा।
पुराणपुरुषा गोविन्दा। पुण्डरीकाक्षा गोविन्दा॥ २॥

गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा। गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा।
गोपीवल्लभ गोविन्दा। गोवर्धनोद्धार गोविन्दा॥ ३॥

दशरथनन्दन गोविन्दा। दशमुखमर्दन गोविन्दा।
पक्षिवाहना गोविन्दा। पाण्डवप्रिय गोविन्दा॥ ४॥

वज्रमकुटधर गोविन्दा। वराहमूर्ति गोविन्दा।
वामनरूपा गोविन्दा। वासुदेवा गोविन्दा॥ ५॥

मत्स्यकूर्मा गोविन्दा। मधुसूदना गोविन्दा।
वृन्दावनचन्द्र गोविन्दा। वेदान्तवेद्य गोविन्दा॥ ६॥

नारायणा गोविन्दा। नरसिंहा गोविन्दा।
श्रीरामा गोविन्दा। श्रीकृष्णा गोविन्दा॥ ७॥

राधावल्लभ गोविन्दा। रमालोला गोविन्दा।
लक्ष्मीवल्लभ गोविन्दा। लक्ष्मीनरसिंह गोविन्दा॥ ८॥

दामोदरा गोविन्दा। दयानिधे गोविन्दा।
पद्मनाभा गोविन्दा। परमपुरुषा गोविन्दा॥ ९॥

तिरुमल वासा गोविन्दा। तिरुपति वेंकटरमणा गोविन्दा।
जगन्नाथा गोविन्दा। जनार्दना गोविन्दा॥ १०॥

श्रीसत्यनारायणा गोविन्दा। श्रीअच्युता गोविन्दा।
श्रीधरा गोविन्दा। श्रीपुरुषोत्तमा गोविन्दा॥ ११॥

आनन्दनिलया गोविन्दा। अनन्तपद्मनाभा गोविन्दा।
केशवा गोविन्दा। केदारनाथ गोविन्दा॥ १२॥

गोविन्दा गोविन्दा श्री वेंकटेशा गोविन्दा।
गोविन्दा गोविन्दा गोविन्दा गोविन्दा॥

॥ इति श्री गोविन्द नामावली सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Govinda Namavali ||

Shrinivasa Govinda | Shri Venkatesha Govinda |
Bhaktavatsala Govinda | Bhagavatapriya Govinda || 1 ||

Nitya-nirmala Govinda | Nila-megha-shyama Govinda |
Purana-purusha Govinda | Pundarikaksha Govinda || 2 ||

Govinda Hari Govinda | Gokula-nandana Govinda |
Gopi-vallabha Govinda | Govardhanoddara Govinda || 3 ||

Dasharatha-nandana Govinda | Dashamukha-mardana Govinda |
Pakshi-vahana Govinda | Pandava-priya Govinda || 4 ||

Vajra-makuta-dhara Govinda | Varaha-murti Govinda |
Vamana-rupa Govinda | Vasudeva Govinda || 5 ||

Matsya-Kurma Govinda | Madhusudana Govinda |
Vrindavana-chandra Govinda | Vedanta-vedya Govinda || 6 ||

Narayana Govinda | Narasimha Govinda |
Shri Rama Govinda | Shri Krishna Govinda || 7 ||

Radha-vallabha Govinda | Rama-lola Govinda |
Lakshmi-vallabha Govinda | Lakshmi-Narasimha Govinda || 8 ||

Damodara Govinda | Daya-nidhe Govinda |
Padmanabha Govinda | Parama-purusha Govinda || 9 ||

Tirumala-vasa Govinda | Tirupati Venkataramana Govinda |
Jagannatha Govinda | Janardana Govinda || 10 ||

Shri Satya-Narayana Govinda | Shri Achyuta Govinda |
Shridhara Govinda | Shri Purushottama Govinda || 11 ||

Ananda-nilaya Govinda | Ananta-Padmanabha Govinda |
Keshava Govinda | Kedaranatha Govinda || 12 ||

Govinda Govinda Shri Venkatesha Govinda |
Govinda Govinda Govinda Govinda ||

|| Iti Shri Govinda Namavali Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्रीनिवास गोविन्दा। श्री वेंकटेश गोविन्दा। भक्तवत्सल गोविन्दा। भागवतप्रिय गोविन्दा॥

नित्य निर्मल गोविन्दा। नीलमेघ श्यामवर्ण गोविन्दा। पुराणपुरुष गोविन्दा। कमलनयन गोविन्दा॥

गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा। गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा। गोपियों के प्रिय गोविन्दा। गोवर्धन उद्धार करने वाले गोविन्दा॥

दशरथनन्दन गोविन्दा। दशमुख (रावण) का मर्दन करने वाले गोविन्दा। गरुड़ वाहन गोविन्दा। पाण्डवप्रिय गोविन्दा॥

वज्रमुकुट धारी गोविन्दा। वराहमूर्ति गोविन्दा। वामन रूप गोविन्दा। वासुदेव गोविन्दा॥

मत्स्य-कूर्म अवतार गोविन्दा। मधुसूदन गोविन्दा। वृन्दावन चन्द्र गोविन्दा। वेदान्त वेद्य गोविन्दा॥

नारायण गोविन्दा। नरसिंह गोविन्दा। श्रीराम गोविन्दा। श्रीकृष्ण गोविन्दा॥

राधावल्लभ गोविन्दा। रमा (लक्ष्मी) के प्रिय गोविन्दा। लक्ष्मीवल्लभ गोविन्दा। लक्ष्मी-नरसिंह गोविन्दा॥

दामोदर गोविन्दा। दया के भण्डार गोविन्दा। पद्मनाभ गोविन्दा। परम पुरुष गोविन्दा॥

तिरुमला निवासी गोविन्दा। तिरुपति वेंकटरमण गोविन्दा। जगन्नाथ गोविन्दा। जनार्दन गोविन्दा॥

श्री सत्यनारायण गोविन्दा। श्री अच्युत गोविन्दा। श्रीधर गोविन्दा। श्री पुरुषोत्तम गोविन्दा॥

आनन्दनिलय गोविन्दा। अनन्तपद्मनाभ गोविन्दा। केशव गोविन्दा। केदारनाथ गोविन्दा॥

गोविन्दा गोविन्दा श्री वेंकटेश गोविन्दा। गोविन्दा गोविन्दा गोविन्दा गोविन्दा॥
benefits: Sacred chant recited during Tirupati pilgrimage
Invokes all divine names and forms of Lord Govinda
Traditionally invoked to establish powerful devotional vibration through repetition
Invokes the blessing of blessings of lord venkateswara
Traditionally associated with joy and spiritual upliftment
description: Govinda Namavali — गोविन्द नामावली — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Krishna. Attributed to Tirupati Temple Tradition, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to purify the mind and environment as well as create powerful devotional vibration through repetition. Additionally, it is known to invoke all divine names and forms of Lord Govinda. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar), Saturday (Shanivar), and Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Vaikuntha Ekadashi and Brahmotsavam. The text contains 12 verses and takes around 10 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Govinda Namavali in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Govinda Namavali?
seoFaqs.answer: The Govinda Namavali (गोविन्द नामावली — literally Garland of Govinda's Names) is the celebrated Sanskrit-Telugu sankirtana of divine names ending with the refrain Govinda, sung continuously by millions of pilgrims as they ascend the seven sacred hills (Saptagiri) and stand in the kilometres-long darshan queue at the Sri Venkateshwara Swami Mandir at Tirumala-Tirupati, Andhra Pradesh — the most visited Hindu temple in the world (an estimated 75,000 pilgrims daily, 50 million annually). The Namavali enumerates the various svarupa-namas of Lord Vishnu-Krishna in his avataras, abodes and qualities — Srinivasa Govinda (the abode of Sri-Lakshmi), Sri Venkatesha Govinda (Lord of Venkata Hill), Bhakta-vatsala Govinda (loving to bhaktas), Nila-megha-shyama Govinda (dark as a rain-cloud), Gopala Govinda, Govardhanoddhara Govinda, Vasudeva Govinda, Tirumala-vasa Govinda, Padmanabha Govinda — each line punctuated by the Govinda call-and-response. (Source: Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanam tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Govinda Govinda chanted at Tirupati?
seoFaqs.answer: The continuous chanting of Govinda Govinda by pilgrims at Tirumala-Tirupati has multiple layered meanings: (1) Govinda is one of the principal names of Sri Krishna-Vishnu, meaning protector of cows (go-vinda), giver of pleasure to cows, knower of the senses, and master of the earth (go also means earth and senses); (2) Sri Venkateshwara of Tirumala is identified by the entire South Indian Vaishnava tradition (especially Sri-Vaishnava Acharya Ramanujacharya) as the supreme Vishnu-Govinda himself, kalau venkata-nayakah — in the Kali yuga the Lord stands at Venkatadri; (3) the rhythmic Govinda call-and-response keeps the long darshan queue spiritually focused, prevents impatience over the multi-hour wait, generates collective bhakti-vibration, and sanctifies the climb up the seven hills; (4) every pilgrim's vow (mokku) at Tirumala traditionally concludes with Govinda — the proof that the wish has reached the Lord. The chant is the auditory signature of Tirumala. (Sources: Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanam; Sri-Vaishnava sampradaya.)

seoFaqs.question: Which divine names appear in the Govinda Namavali?
seoFaqs.answer: The Govinda Namavali enumerates a comprehensive set of Vishnu-Krishna names organised by category: (1) Tirumala-specific — Srinivasa Govinda, Sri Venkatesha Govinda, Tirumala-vasa Govinda, Tirupati Venkata-ramana Govinda, Ananda-nilaya Govinda; (2) Avatara names — Matsya-Kurma Govinda, Varaha-murti Govinda, Vamana-rupa Govinda, Narasimha Govinda, Sri Rama Govinda, Sri Krishna Govinda; (3) Vrindavana-leela — Gokula-nandana Govinda, Gopi-vallabha Govinda, Govardhanoddhara Govinda, Vrindavana-chandra Govinda; (4) Conjugal — Radha-vallabha Govinda, Lakshmi-vallabha Govinda, Rama-lola Govinda, Lakshmi-Narasimha Govinda; (5) Cosmic-philosophical — Purana-purusha Govinda, Pundarikaksha Govinda, Vedanta-vedya Govinda, Sat-Chid-Ananda-rupa Govinda, Parama-purusha Govinda; (6) Maha-Vishnu — Damodara Govinda, Padmanabha Govinda, Janardana Govinda, Achyuta Govinda, Shri Purushottama Govinda, Keshava Govinda. (Source: Tirumala Tirupati seva-paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Govinda Namavali be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Govinda Namavali is most powerful on Vaikuntha Ekadashi (Margashirsha / Pausha Shukla Ekadashi — the supreme Vishnu Ekadashi when the Vaikuntha-dvara at Tirumala is opened for special darshan, considered equal to attaining Vaikuntha after death), Sri Venkateshwara Brahmotsavam (the nine-day annual festival in Ashvin month with the famous Garuda-vahana, Sesha-vahana and Hanumantha-vahana processions), Tirupati Bramhotsavam, all Saturdays (Shanivar — sacred to Lord Venkateshwara), Ekadashis, Anuradha Nakshatra days, Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar). Daily recitation in the morning facing east, especially before commencing a Tirumala pilgrimage, before any major undertaking, after a vow-fulfilment at Tirumala, or as an accompaniment to deep-pradakshina around any Vishnu temple, is the universal South Indian practice. The Namavali is also the standard chant during temple processional utsavas. (Sources: Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanam; Sri-Vaishnava festival calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Govinda Namavali grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere recitation of the Govinda Namavali: (1) the protection and prosperity of Sri Venkateshwara, who is by Sri-Vaishnava theology the Kali-yuga avatara of Vishnu specifically descended for the welfare of devotees in this age, (2) fulfilment of all vows (mokku-prarthanas) made at Tirumala — financial, marital, child-birth, health, career, education, (3) the merit of nama-sankirtana, declared by Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu to be the supreme yuga-dharma of Kali-yuga (kalau nasti eva nasti eva nasti eva gatir-anyatha — there is no other path), (4) purification of mind and environment through repeated nama-uchcharana, (5) joy and spiritual upliftment from the rhythmic call-and-response, (6) blessings of Sri-Lakshmi who is consort of Govinda (Srinivasa = abode of Sri), (7) liberation through Vishnu-bhakti, with Vaikuntha-prapti at the time of death, especially if one has chanted Govinda continuously during life. (Source: Tirumala-tirupati phala-shruti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Govinda Namavali and the Vishnu Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are sacred Vishnu-name compositions but differ in scriptural source, length, format and primary use. The Vishnu Sahasranama is the canonical 1000-name Sanskrit stotra from the Anushasana Parva of the Mahabharata, taught by Bhishma to Yudhishthira from his bed of arrows, in continuous shloka-form metre with each name in genitive/accusative case — recited as a 30-40 minute path by Smarta and Vaishnava brahmanas as the supreme nitya-parayana, with Adi Shankaracharya, Parashara Bhatta and Madhva Acharya all having written elaborate bhashyas on it. The Govinda Namavali is a much shorter, simpler call-and-response sankirtana from the Tirumala Tirupati temple tradition, with each name ending in the refrain Govinda, designed for collective public chanting in the darshan queue, on pilgrimage, and in temple processions — accessible to children, illiterate devotees and non-Sanskrit speakers. Many devotees recite both: Vishnu Sahasranama for nitya-path, Govinda Namavali for sankirtana. (Sources: Mahabharata; Tirumala Tirupati seva-paddhati.)
